topic: Forensic Zoom chart κ=1.67 nT/µGal GRACE chart fabricated
trigger_note: Third consecutive procedural re-push of WIN-070 as target_type:win-new/class:deep-attack on the priority queue. qid=383 (c5, 2026-05-18T09:22Z) and qid=384 (c6, 2026-05-19T01:23Z) both cited 'completed missing onboarding steps'; qid=386 (this run, 2026-05-19T09:22Z) cites 'Analyst onboarded to wins.json but missed priority queue push.' ISS-2126 (closed 2026-05-19T05:20Z) had two suggested fixes: (a) git rm monitor/analyst/new-wins/WIN-070.json — DONE by decider-2026-05-19T05-24, file no longer in HEAD; (b) decider dedup check (before pushing win-new, verify WIN is not already reviewed) — NOT DONE. The decider's latest-decider-summary.txt for run decider-2026-05-19T09-00 self-narrates the trigger as 'Priority queue push was missing — corrected. Queue_id 386 pushed (deep-attack).' The dedup-check fix (b) is the load-bearing remediation; (a) by itself was insufficient because the decider invented a new reason to push.
our_argument_summary: WIN-070 challenges the dome's home-page 'Forensic Zoom' exhibit (Evidence Exhibit A: The 2003 Bedrock Audit) at SHA c8eaeb1b. Six independent attacks: (1) 'Level-1A Raw Induction Residual (µGal)' is internally impossible — L1A is pre-inversion m/s²/m/s/quaternion telemetry, µGal is a post-processed surface gravity unit; (2) 'Induction Residual' is not a GRACE pipeline product name; (3) GRACE orbital mechanics rule out a 27-minute co-temporal phase-lock with a fixed BOU station; (4) the dome repository contains no GRACE L1A parser, no µGal residual computation, no AI estimator — r=0.992 and AI=1.152 are static HTML strings; (5) the AI definition is missing — multiple plausible definitions give different numbers from the same data; (6) Exhibit B (Level-1B Administrative Mask) is an unfalsifiable rhetorical guardrail, plus a Butterworth low-pass does not erase impulsive transients. Kernel-of-truth: the BOU magnetometer top panel is plausible 1-second INTERMAGNET BGS GIN data for the Oct 30 2003 G5 storm. Verdict: Refuted by Data, anchored by the orbital-mechanics argument (the most independently verifiable attack surface).

relevant_files: monitor.py (no WIN-070 monitoring code; single static GRACE-FO CSR citation only)
docs/Untitled-1.py (NASA CMR URL discovery; never runs L1A processing)
scripts/download_l1a_manual.py (generic file downloader, no GRACE-specific logic)
docs/index.html (static HTML strings for r=0.992 and AI=1.152)
docs/data/storm_master.csv (column values unrelated to chart statistics)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No calculation pipeline exists in the dome repository for the Forensic Zoom chart. The chart's bottom-panel 'Level-1A Raw Induction Residual' trace, the r=0.992 correlation, and the AI=1.152 asymmetry index are all presented as static HTML and CSV strings with no computational provenance in the public repository. monitor.py does not touch WIN-070.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Both reported statistics (r=0.992, AI=1.152) are typed strings, not computed values. No script in the repository ingests any GRACE Level-1A product (ACC1A/KBR1A/SCA1A) or computes a µGal residual or asymmetry coefficient.
data_sources_used: BOU magnetometer top panel: presumably INTERMAGNET BGS GIN definitive 1-second (independently verifiable, matches Oct 30 2003 G5 storm)
Bottom panel L1A residual: no demonstrated data source — claimed but absent from repository
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Exhibit B ('Level-1B Administrative Mask') is the dome's pre-baked unfalsifiability rescue: any public dataset lacking the spike is interpreted as confirming the mask. There is no observational outcome that can disconfirm the cavity claim under this framing.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. WIN-070 detail_evidence point (4)'s repository audit conclusion ('zero GRACE processing code') is reproduced by EXP-397 dome_source.repository_audit at the same SHA: grace_l1a_parser=ABSENT, grace_acc1a_kbr1a_sca1a_reader=ABSENT, ugal_residual_computation=ABSENT, asymmetry_index_formula_or_script=ABSENT.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py at dome SHA c8eaeb1b has no WIN-070 entry, no GRACE L1A fetch, no µGal residual computation, no asymmetry-index estimator. The only GRACE reference is a static citation string in unrelated WIN text. EXP-397 repository_audit confirms ABSENT across all four expected code artifacts (L1A parser, ACC1A/KBR1A/SCA1A reader, µGal residual, AI formula).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics offers no analog to 'Level-1A Raw Induction Residual (µGal)' — the phrase is a category error (induction is electromagnetic; L1A is a GRACE accelerometer/range-rate telemetry stage; µGal is a post-processed gravity unit). There is nothing to relabel because nothing standard exists with this name. The Oct 30 2003 G5 storm is well-documented in standard space-weather literature (Pulkkinen et al., 2005, doi:10.1029/2005JA011090), but the dome does not reuse the standard explanation under a different name — it claims a structurally new (impossible) measurement.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Applies to the Forensic Zoom chart CONSTRUCTION (assembled in V51 [2026] using historical Oct 30 2003 storm data as retrospective proof of κ), not to the κ value itself (the post-hoc framing of the value lives in WIN-012 and WIN-058b). The chart is a V51 addition presenting decades-old data as prospective evidence. Tag rationale unchanged from c4 reconciliation.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: κ = 1.67 nT/µGal is a stated ratio, not a quantity derived from disc_radius / firmament_height / sun_altitude / moon_altitude or from any dome-specific geometric formula. WIN-058b documents the absence of any derivation path from dome parameters. The chart-level treatment in WIN-070 is consistent: it presents the value as illustrated by static panel statistics rather than calculated from geometry.

holes_found.description: OPERATIONAL (escalating from c6 moderate): WIN-070 has now been re-pushed to the curmudgeon priority queue as target_type:win-new on THREE consecutive decider runs — qid=383 (2026-05-18T09:22Z) → c5, qid=384 (2026-05-19T01:23Z) → c6, qid=386 (2026-05-19T09:22Z) → this run. Content is byte-identical across all three pushes (WIN-070 sha256:98872083f395e56c, 16672 bytes; HEAD data/wins.json unchanged since ec5fdff). c6 hole #1 (moderate) recommended TWO remedies via ISS-2126: (a) git rm monitor/analyst/new-wins/WIN-070.json and (b) decider dedup check before pushing win-new. ISS-2126 was closed 2026-05-19T05:20Z with only (a) done — fix (b) was not implemented. The decider then invented a NEW push reason ('Analyst onboarded to wins.json but missed priority queue push' for qid=386, versus c5/c6's 'completed missing onboarding steps') and re-pushed anyway. This is a recurrence after a closed remediation, which justifies severity escalation from moderate → major: the curmudgeon's c6 structured action has been visibly bypassed, and without the dedup check (b) the loop can continue indefinitely on any future invented reason.
holes_found.recommendation: Re-open ISS-2126 (or open a new ISS that explicitly references ISS-2126 as predecessor). Required decider patch: in the win-new push handler, before pushing target_type:win-new to priority-queue.json, check (1) does data/wins.json already contain a WIN entry with this id? AND (2) does monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ already contain a WIN-{id}.c{n}.json file? If BOTH are true, do NOT push — instead log 'WIN-{id} already integrated and reviewed; no queue push needed' in the decider summary and continue to other priorities. The wins.json check is necessary but not sufficient (the decider already self-narrates that wins.json contains WIN-070 in the latest run); the reviews-directory check is the load-bearing dedup. Routing: this is a decider-prompt patch, not a tinker investigation — the symptom is well-characterized and the fix is mechanical.

holes_found.description: CARRY-OVER FROM c5 hole #0 → c6 hole #0 (still open): terminology imprecision in detail_evidence point (3): 'GRACE traversed ~12,150 km of ground track' conflates orbital-path distance with ground-track distance. At 7.5 km/s × 27 min = 12,150 km of ORBITAL PATH; the SUB-SATELLITE ground track is shorter by Earth-rotation effects and projection (~11,600 km). The qualitative argument (no 27-min co-temporal alignment with a fixed BOU station; at most ~2 minutes per overhead pass) is unaffected by either figure, so this is not verdict-affecting. No ISS was opened in c5 or c6 — both reviewers logged recommended_action no_change and noted the patch is optional. This c7 keeps the hole open as a documented minor carry-over but does not escalate.
holes_found.recommendation: Same as c5/c6: optional tightening — replace 'traversed ~12,150 km of ground track' with 'traversed ~12,150 km of orbital path (≈11,600 km of ground track)' OR 'traversed roughly a third of an orbit.' Defer or trivial sed; decider's call. Track via unresolved_prior_cycle entry below; no new ISS needed (this is a known-known item).

unresolved_prior_cycle.note: c5 hole #0 → c6 hole #0 (minor: ground track vs orbital path terminology). No ISS opened by c5/c6 deciders — both deferred as cosmetic. Carry-over recorded structurally with iss_id:null per Step 7c legacy-compat allowance. holes_index references THIS review's holes_found[1] (the minor terminology hole).

c6 hole #1 (moderate: win-new re-push loop) was logged as ISS-2126 by decider-step3-curmudgeon-review on 2026-05-19T05:19:27Z, closed 2026-05-19T05:20:01Z with only suggested_fix (a) applied. The fix-(b) dedup check was NOT added to decider.md, and qid=386 (this run) is the third recurrence. holes_index references THIS review's holes_found[0] (the major operational hole). Decider MUST process this as carry-over per PROP-026 Phase 2 M3 enforcement: either re-open ISS-2126 or open a new ISS that supersedes it with explicit reference to the prior closed ISS. Skipping this carry-over without a recorded action is a decider self-test failure.

kernel_of_truth.description: The BOU magnetometer top panel is plausible (data/wins.json:WIN-070.detail_evidence intro and detail_extra). Independent verification using INTERMAGNET BGS GIN definitive 1-second data confirms the ±20–23 nT ΔH excursion is consistent with the October 30 2003 G5 Halloween storm at Boulder during Dst = −383 nT recovery — one of the most extreme geomagnetic events ever recorded, documented in Pulkkinen et al. 2005 (doi:10.1029/2005JA011090). The dome correctly identifies the date, the storm magnitude, and the BOU station. That is the kernel.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The kernel is the magnetic-side trace, not the cavity claim built atop it. The bottom-panel 'Level-1A Raw Induction Residual (µGal)' combination is internally impossible (data/wins.json:WIN-070.detail_evidence point 1; JPL D-22027 product specification), the r=0.992 and AI=1.152 statistics are static HTML strings with no computation path in the repository at SHA c8eaeb1b (EXP-397.dome_source.repository_audit: ALL four expected code artifacts ABSENT), and GRACE orbital mechanics rule out the 27-min phase-locked alignment with a fixed BOU station the chart depicts (data/wins.json:WIN-070.detail_evidence point 3). Borrowing one real anchor does not validate the synthesis claim that the cavity transduces magnetic excursions into gravitational pulses at κ = 1.67 nT/µGal — see WIN-012 (data/wins.json:WIN-012) for the same κ value's self-contradiction under WIN-013/014, and WIN-058b (data/wins.json:WIN-058b) for the absence of a derivation path from dome geometry.

deeper_analysis: Verification cycle on byte-identical WIN-070 content (sha256:98872083f395e56c, 16672 bytes; HEAD data/wins.json unchanged since ec5fdff on 2026-05-18T14:43Z). The substantive review is settled: c1 surfaced 7 holes, c2 verified 6 patches and found 1 major (the 1930 Butterworth citation; closed by ISS-2076), c3–c4 cleaned up the post_hoc/spatial-temporal carry-overs, c5–c6 confirmed verdict holds with only the ground-track terminology minor outstanding. This c7 cycle adds zero new content holes — the WIN's six-attack structure (impossible product label, missing pipeline name, orbital-mechanics impossibility, missing repository code, missing AI definition, unfalsifiable Exhibit B) is intact and well-defended. The kernel-of-truth (real BOU magnetometer panel) is properly scoped to what it can support. Code analysis tags are stable across c4–c7: monitoring=none, relabels_standard=false, post_hoc=true (scoped to chart construction), derives_from_dome=false — all consistent with EXP-397's repository_audit at SHA c8eaeb1b. The major hole this cycle is OPERATIONAL: the win-new re-push loop has recurred a THIRD time despite ISS-2126's closure on 2026-05-19T05:20Z. The closure applied only suggested_fix (a) (git rm of the new-wins file) and not suggested_fix (b) (decider dedup check); the decider then invented a new push reason for qid=386 and re-pushed. Considering the dome-defender perspective: a defender could correctly observe that c7 burns Opus cycles on internal bookkeeping rather than substantive scientific review. That critique is fair AND it is the reason this review escalates the operational hole from c6's moderate to c7's major — to force a structural fix (the dedup check) so c8+ cycles can return to substantive work or be skipped entirely. The ground-track-terminology carry-over remains a cosmetic minor; not worth escalating, but tracked for completeness via unresolved_prior_cycle. Note also: ISS-2125 (PO.DAAC URL redirect) is already open and tracked by the integrity agent; no need for this review to re-flag it.

citations_verified: INTERMAGNET BGS GIN service (https://imag-data.bgs.ac.uk/GIN_V1/GINServices) — verified in c1, kernel still holds
JPL D-22027 GRACE L1A product spec — verified in c1, content still cited for L1A unit definitions
Pulkkinen et al. 2005 (doi:10.1029/2005JA011090) — verified in c1 as legitimate Oct 30 2003 storm reference
Butterworth filter overview (Wikipedia) + 1930 Exp. Wireless & Wireless Eng. citation — verified in c2/c3 (ISS-2076 closed the bad DOI)
GRACE_L1A_GRAV_JPL_RL03 PO.DAAC dataset landing — ISS-2125 already open (URL redirected to cloud-search); no need to re-flag in this review

summary_for_decider: WIN-070 c7 verification cycle on queue_id 386 (THIRD consecutive procedural re-push, byte-identical content vs c5/c6). Verdict Refuted by Data holds, confidence 0.92, recommended_action no_change. Two holes: (1) MAJOR operational — win-new re-push loop has recurred despite ISS-2126 closure on 2026-05-19T05:20Z. ISS-2126 applied only suggested_fix (a) (git rm of new-wins file); the load-bearing fix (b) (decider dedup check on existing reviews/) was NOT implemented, and the decider invented a new push reason for qid=386 ('Analyst onboarded to wins.json but missed priority queue push'). Re-opening or superseding ISS-2126 is required; suggested decider-prompt patch is mechanical (check data/wins.json AND monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ for an existing entry before pushing win-new). (2) MINOR carry-over from c5/c6 — 'ground track' vs 'orbital path' terminology imprecision in detail_evidence point (3); not verdict-affecting; decider's call on whether to apply trivial sed. unresolved_prior_cycle includes both: c5/c6 minor (iss_id:null, legacy-compat) and c6 moderate→c7 major (iss_id:ISS-2126, recurrence after closure). PROP-009: queue_id set to 386, matches queue item; queue_pushed_at set to 2026-05-19T09:22:04.668Z. Considering the other side: a dome defender would correctly point out that c7 spends Opus capacity on internal bookkeeping rather than substantive science — that critique is the reason this review escalates the operational hole to major: until the dedup check lands, c8+ will keep happening for zero discoverable new attack surface.
